terms.definition: Large, landed estates specializing in cash-crop agriculture like grain and olives, often worked by enslaved labor.
Christians who faltered under persecution and denied the faith or handed over Scriptures, sparking intense debate over their restoration.
Clergy who surrendered copies of Scripture to Roman authorities to be burned during persecution; literally 'handers-over'.
A rigorist schism in North Africa that argued sacraments performed by compromised clergy (traditores) were invalid.
A heresy that denied original sin and taught humans could achieve righteousness by their own moral effort, heavily opposed by Augustine.
A heresy embraced by the Vandals that denied the full, co-equal divinity of Jesus Christ with God the Father.
